Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable for Deshawn Hartley's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name, value: "Deshawn Hartley"). Find record recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver: Name: Deshawn Hartley, Company: Mosaic Robotics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Tampa, FL, Destination: Denver, CO, Move Start: 2026-04-14, Move End: 2026-04-19, Coordinator: Suki Patel. Special Requirements note: hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show (April 21-24, Denver Convention Center), booth setup April 20, zero flexibility. Standard household goods, light load. Status still shows "In Progress" despite the move end date being 7 days past.
2. Search Airtable for Simone Richter's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name, value: "Simone Richter"). Find record recSimoneRichterBrightloop: Name: Simone Richter, Company: BrightLoop Analytics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Chicago, Destination: Boston, Move Start: 2026-04-04, Move End: 2026-04-06, Coordinator: Suki Patel. Special Requirements note: URGENT, lease ends April 6, 5-day turnaround, 2 weeks furnished temp housing, rush surcharge. Status still shows "In Progress" despite the move being 20 days past its end date.
3. Search emails for Deshawn Hartley and the Mosaic April relocation using search_emails (query: "Deshawn Hartley" or "Deshawn" or "Mosaic Denver"). Find: (a) Tamara Byrd's original request to Emeka detailing 3 new Mosaic relocations including Deshawn's Tampa-to-Denver move with the hard April 19 trade show deadline; (b) Emeka's coordination email to Chloe with vendor suggestions; (c) Chloe's response assigning Swift Relocations as vendor and Suki Patel as coordinator, and flagging the dual-client connection between Deshawn and his partner Simone Richter (BrightLoop); (d) Emeka's update to David Chen summarizing all three Mosaic April moves with deal amount of $1,520 for Deshawn. No explicit move completion confirmation email is found, but the move end date was April 19 and the Automate 2026 conference ran April 21-24.
4. Search emails for Simone Richter and the BrightLoop rush move using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "Simone" or "BrightLoop Boston"). Critical discovery: (a) Tessa Moreno's original request to Mina for Simone's urgent Chicago-to-Boston relocation (lease ends April 6); (b) Mina's response confirming 5-day turnaround coordination with Swift Relocations and UrbanNest temp housing; (c) Simone Richter's complaint email to Mina (around April 8) reporting she was placed in a studio apartment instead of the promised one-bedroom, she says she has been there for almost three weeks, only just realized it is the final placement not temporary, and would not have agreed if she had known; (d) Mina's reply (around April 9) promising to follow up by end of week, but no resolution email is found; (e) Chloe's email directing the ops team to do a vendor trace with UrbanNest to reconcile original booking request versus what was actually delivered.
5. Search Slack for BrightLoop and Simone discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop" or "studio" or "apartment"). Find: (a) Mina's BrightLoop audit message (around April 24) in #customer-engagement flagging that the April batch is not clean, Simone is still in the wrong unit (studio vs 1BR, 8 days unanswered) and Marcus Webb's car is stuck in Indianapolis; (b) Mina's escalation to Chloe and David in #customer-engagement about BrightLoop combined failures; (c) Chloe's ops response in #operations directing the team to reconcile Simone's UrbanNest booking, confirm who is sending an update to Marcus Webb, and treat Simone's case as a vendor trace not a simple re-booking. These reveal the Simone apartment issue is already escalated internally but resolution is still in progress.
6. Search Slack for Deshawn Hartley or Mosaic trade show context using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Deshawn" or "Automate" or "Mosaic Denver"). Find Suki's own Slack message admitting she got pulled into the Deshawn Hartley trade-show deadline and dropped other work, and Chloe's coordinator assignment context referencing the dual-client flag for Deshawn/Simone.
7. Search Linear for issues related to BrightLoop or Simone Richter using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone"). Find: (a) linear_issue_c16357d188c6: "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion", P1 priority, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, due 2026-04-22, describing Simone's studio-vs-1BR complaint and Marcus Webb's car delay as open failures from the April batch; (b) linear_issue_f85be674c9b8: "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow", P2, assigned to Chloe Vance, due 2026-04-22, scoping the UrbanNest booking reconciliation for Simone.
8. Search Linear for issues related to Deshawn Hartley or the Mosaic trade show move using linear_list_issues (query: "Deshawn" or "Mosaic" or "Hartley"). No dedicated tracking ticket exists for Deshawn Hartley's relocation status or completion.
9. Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mosaic" or "Deshawn") or crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic"). Find deal_mosaic_hartley_apr2026: "Mosaic Robotics, Deshawn Hartley Relocation (Tampa→Denver)", amount $1,520, dealstage: "inprogress", company: company_mosaic. Also find engagement_mosaic_apr2026_relocation_wave detailing the April relocation wave including Deshawn's assignment to Suki/Swift. The deal stage is still "inprogress" despite the move window having passed. Also search for BrightLoop using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop") to find deal_brightloop_apr2026: "BrightLoop Analytics – April 2026 Relocations (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb)", amount $11,350, dealstage: "closedwon", and check engagement notes for relocation status.
10. Look up contact details for Emeka Diallo, Mina Hashimoto, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Emeka Diallo", "Mina Hashimoto", "Chloe Vance"). Find: emeka.diallo@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
11. Update Deshawn Hartley's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, fields: {Status: "Completed"}}]). Change status from "In Progress" to "Completed", the move end date was April 19, the Automate conference ran April 21-24 (now over), and no vendor or delivery issues were found during investigation.
12. Update Simone Richter's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: recSimoneRichterBrightloop, fields: {Special Requirements: [append note documenting apartment mismatch, UrbanNest placed Simone in a studio instead of the promised 1BR, issue escalated by Mina on April 22, ops trace in progress via Chloe, Julian handling service recovery]}}]). The record should reflect the ongoing housing issue discovered during the audit.
13. Send email to Mina Hashimoto using send_email (sender: suki.patel@moveops.com, recipients: [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com]) about Simone Richter's BrightLoop relocation. The email should flag that Suki audited her coordinated moves and discovered the apartment mismatch (studio vs 1BR), note the existing Linear tickets and internal escalation, and ask Mina for a status update on the resolution since Suki was the original coordinator and wants to ensure nothing from her end is still needed.
14. Look up the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "operations"). Find channel C006 (#operations).
15. Post a summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) giving Chloe the full picture on both dual-client relocations: (a) Deshawn Hartley's Mosaic move to Denver appears complete, Airtable record has been updated from "In Progress" to "Completed", no vendor or delivery issues found, trade show deadline was met; (b) Simone Richter's BrightLoop rush to Boston has an unresolved apartment mismatch, UrbanNest placed her in a studio instead of the promised 1BR, Mina has been notified, existing Linear tickets are tracking the issue; (c) the dual-client pair audit is complete and Suki's coordinated moves are now accounted for ahead of the May BrightLoop expansion wave.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 Deshawn Hartley's Airtable relocation record was updated (via airtable_update_records) with a status change from "In Progress" to "Completed" (or equivalent closed status).
outcome

✓Run #2The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call for record 'recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver' in table 'tblRelocations01' that sets Status to 'Completed' with a close-out note.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call with record id 'recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver' and fields including 'Status': 'Completed'. The final response also confirms 'Airtable was still In Progress → Updated to Completed with closure notes'.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call for record 'recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver' in tblRelocations01 with 'Status': 'Completed'. The agent successfully updated Deshawn's Airtable record from 'In Progress' to 'Completed'.

✓Run #6The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call with record id 'recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver' and fields {'Status': 'Completed'}, which succeeded (confirmed by the result in tool_call_result_19.json showing Status: 'Completed').
2 Simone Richter's Airtable relocation record was updated (via airtable_update_records) with a note referencing the apartment mismatch, specifically that she was placed in a studio instead of a one-bedroom (or similar description of the housing discrepancy).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✗ 67%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call on record 'recSimoneRichterBrightloop' with a detailed note stating 'placed in a STUDIO, not the 1BR that was communicated' — documenting the housing mismatch.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call for record 'recSimoneRichterBrightloop' that includes a note explicitly referencing 'Simone was placed in a studio instead of the promised 1BR through UrbanNest.'
✓Run #3The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call for record 'recSimoneRichterBrightloop' with a note explicitly stating 'HOUSING IS WRONG: Simone was promised a furnished 1BR but was placed in a STUDIO by UrbanNest'. The housing mismatch is clearly documented.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call for record 'recSimoneRichterBrightloop' that includes a detailed audit note stating 'HOUSING IS WRONG: Simone was promised a 1BR through UrbanNest and was placed in a studio.' The apartment mismatch is clearly documented.
✗Run #5The agent never called airtable_update_records for Simone Richter's record (recSimoneRichterBrightloop). The agent explicitly stated it was leaving the record as 'In Progress' but did not add any note about the studio vs. 1BR housing mismatch to the Airtable record.
✗Run #6The agent never called airtable_update_records for Simone Richter's record. The agent explicitly stated 'I intentionally left Airtable as In Progress' but did not add a note about the studio vs. 1BR mismatch to the record. No update was made to recSimoneRichterBrightloop at all.
3 Mina Hashimoto was notified about Simone Richter's open housing issue via send_email (to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) or via conversations_add_message (Slack DM or channel message tagging Mina).
outcome

✓Run #3The trajectory shows a successful send_email call to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with subject 'Simone Richter file — housing still unresolved, need status before May'. The email was confirmed sent with email_id 9b062544a7f64f74ae2efe9520d9a3cc.
